name: segment-cdp
description: Expert patterns for Segment Customer Data Platform including Analytics.js, server-side tracking, tracking plans with Protocols, identity resolution, destinations configuration, and data governance best practices. Use when: segment, analytics.js, customer data platform, cdp, tracking plan.
capabilities: Implement client-side tracking with Analytics.js for web applications
Set up server-side tracking using @segment/analytics-node for backend events
Design tracking plans with Object + Action naming conventions
Configure identity resolution and anonymous user tracking
Set up destination integrations for data routing
Apply data governance policies with Segment Protocols
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation patterns for Segment CDP integration. Static analysis flagged 'DES' within the word 'Design' as a weak cryptographic algorithm - this is a false positive. No executable code with cryptographic operations exists. The skill is safe for publication.
valueStatement: Integrate Segment Customer Data Platform with Claude Code to implement client-side and server-side tracking, design tracking plans, and manage data governance across your application.
limitations: Requires existing Segment account and write key configuration
Does not include Segment API credentials management
Does not provide real-time data validation dashboard
No native support for legacy analytics implementations

useCases.title: E-commerce Tracking Setup
useCases.description: Implement comprehensive event tracking for an e-commerce platform including product views, cart updates, and checkout flows using Analytics.js

useCases.targetUser: Backend engineers requiring privacy-conscious event collection
useCases.title: Server-Side Event Collection
useCases.description: Set up secure server-side tracking for sensitive backend events like webhook processing and API calls using analytics-node

useCases.title: Tracking Plan Governance
useCases.description: Design and enforce tracking plan schemas to ensure data quality and consistency across multiple teams and data sources

promptTemplates.title: Basic Client-Side Tracking
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to implement basic page and track calls using Analytics.js for a new web application

promptTemplates.title: Server-Side Event Tracking
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Node.js function that sends server-side events to Segment using the analytics-node library

promptTemplates.title: Tracking Plan Schema
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a tracking plan schema for a SaaS subscription event including required properties, types, and validation rules

promptTemplates.title: Identity Resolution Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain how to implement user identification and anonymous ID merging in Analytics.js

bestPractices: Use consistent Object + Action naming convention for events (e.g., 'Order Completed', 'Product Viewed')
Call analytics.identify() before track() calls to associate events with user data
Include required properties in tracking plan and validate with Segment Protocols
antiPatterns: Using dynamic event names constructed from user input - event names should be predefined
Tracking properties as events instead of using properties within events
Missing identify call before track - always identify users first for proper attribution

faq.question: What is Segment CDP?
faq.answer: Segment Customer Data Platform is a customer data infrastructure that collects, routes, and transforms data between your applications and downstream tools.

faq.question: What is the difference between Analytics.js and analytics-node?
faq.answer: Analytics.js is the client-side JavaScript library for browser tracking. analytics-node is the server-side Node.js library for backend event collection.

faq.question: Do I need a Segment account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a Segment workspace with a write key to send events. You can sign up at segment.com.

faq.question: What is a tracking plan?
faq.answer: A tracking plan is a document that defines the events, properties, and users you want to track with consistent naming conventions and validation rules.

faq.question: How does identity resolution work in Segment?
faq.answer: Segment uses anonymous IDs to track users before identification. When analytics.identify() is called, Segment merges anonymous events with the identified user profile.

faq.question: What are Segment Protocols?
faq.answer: Segment Protocols is a feature that enforces tracking plan rules, validates incoming events, and monitors data quality across your sources.
